mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline when FEDFUNDS declines over consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE breaches the 4.5% threshold in consecutive observations.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer credit balances and elevated policy rates indicates unsustainable debt-fueled demand that will soon contract, leading to earnings downgrades and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and high FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, industrial production growth becomes the dominant driver of equity returns because capacity constraints amplify profit margins; strong INDPRO sustains positive SPX returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that translates directly into higher corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 jobs signals an abrupt cooling in aggregate demand, prompting equity repricing and negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative following months where PAYEMS declines by more than 50,000 jobs.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production outpaces housing starts by a widening margin, it indicates a rotation from interest-rate-sensitive construction toward durable goods manufacturing, supporting positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The relative strength of INDPRO versus HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation decelerates while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, the resulting peak in real interest rates typically precedes a monetary pivot that compresses discount rates and lifts equity valuations over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not significantly outperform when CPIAUCSL decelerates under high FEDFUNDS conditions (t-test p > 0.05).
